resposta:
solution:
```
class Solution:
    def checkInclusion(self, s1: str, s2: str) -> bool:
        from collections import Counter
        s1_count = Counter(s1)
        for i in range(len(s2) - len(s1) + 1):
            if Counter(s2[i:i+len(s1)]) == s1_count:
                return True
        return False
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(N)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: This solution uses a sliding window approach with a Counter to efficiently check for permutations of s1 in s2.
